result.lens: Upper-bound inversion: if chi = 6, why can nobody color the plane? Characterize the forced pathologies of any 6-coloring (aperiodicity, non-map structure, positive defect density for all periodic attempts) and attack the periodic sector NOW via quotient graphs of the plane, where the W3 identification step is free.
result.first_principles:
Where does Euclidean content live on the UPPER-bound side? Read it off the three controls. In L^infty, chi = 4 is attained by a periodic MAP coloring (half-open unit squares). It works because the L^infty sphere has flat facets that can be laid parallel to tile boundaries, so the distance-1 constraint degenerates along seams. The Euclidean circle is strictly convex with nonvanishing curvature: it meets every rectifiable tile boundary transversally, and O(2) leaves no direction to align with. That transversality is the engine of Townsend/Voronov (map colorings need 7) and of Exoo's epsilon-thickened graphs (every finite-resolution conservative discretization is conjecturally 7-chromatic). So if chi = 6, the witness is invisible to every tile-based, finite-resolution search humans have run. Principle 1: the 7-vs-6 gap from above is carried by curvature plus rotation invariance, the two features absent in L^infty and R^1.

In R^1, periodic interval colorings are optimal because the unit sphere is two points; one period vector satisfies both constraint directions. In R^2 the constraint set is a full circle and a lattice can align with only countably many of its directions; equidistribution of unit directions against any lattice is total. Principle 2: periodicity is in structural tension with O(2); conjecture the tension is maximal: no doubly periodic 6-coloring exists at all, so every flat torus R^2/L is 7-chromatic.

Q^2 enters as discipline, not target: quotient lower bounds must inherit rigidity from spindle-bearing seeds in Q(sqrt3, sqrt11), never from grid combinatorics, and the Fourier step must consume the full circle of unit directions (which Q^2 lacks).

The actionable inversion: the program's wall is W3, realizing an identification of a forced pair inside R^2. In R^2/L identification is FREE: pick lattice generator v = s - t and s,t become one point, plus a whole orbit of identifications and a wrap-edge interface of width >= 2 (exactly the L55 route (ii) shape). Price paid: the conclusion weakens to "no L-periodic 6-coloring". That purchase is exactly what L57 proved unbuyable in the plane.

conjectures.name: C1: Aperiodic Chromatic Rigidity (the einstein phenomenon for colorings)
conjectures.statement: For every rank-2 lattice L < R^2 containing no vector of length exactly 1 (loop-free case), the flat-torus unit-distance graph T_L (vertices R^2/L; edges {x,y} iff |x - y - l| = 1 for some l in L) satisfies chi(T_L) >= 7. Equivalently: there is NO doubly periodic proper 6-coloring of the plane, even with arbitrary non-measurable color classes. Corollary: if chi(R^2) = 6, then every proper 6-coloring of the plane is aperiodic.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Two-sided. Refutation: exhibit any L-periodic 6-coloring; in particular, a SAT-found 6-coloring of a conservative cell discretization of any single torus (cells conflict iff their Minkowski difference meets the unit circle mod L; exact rational cell bounds, python-sat) would refute C1 outright and refute Exoo's epsilon conjecture simultaneously. Confirmation at fixed L: by de Bruijn-Erdos on the torus, chi(T_L) >= 7 iff some FINITE toroidal UDG (finite point set, edges = unit mod L, sympy-exact) is 7-chromatic, a python-sat UNSAT certificate. The repo can start both today.
conjectures.why_plausible: The hex 7-coloring is periodic, and 75 years of periodic search never produced 6. Every conservative discretization of a torus is an Exoo-type [1-eps,1+eps] graph, conjectured 7-chromatic for all eps > 0. Voronov-type results already eliminate all 'nice' (map) periodic colorings; what remains are wild fundamental-domain classes, and for the measurable ones the spectrum is pure point on the dual lattice where the Bochner/J_0 machinery becomes finite (C4). First-principles: a lattice singles out countably many directions while the constraint circle is rotation-equidistributed; O(2) versus L is a genuine incompatibility, the same one that makes the monotile aperiodic.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: chi(R^2) could be 6 with a periodic witness of enormous period and non-measurable classes inside the fundamental domain; nothing today excludes that. The universal quantifier over L is vast: C1 could hold for generic L yet fail for special lattices commensurate with an exotic 6-way recombination of the hex 7-pattern. Also chi(T_L) >= 7 for ALL L is strictly stronger than chi(R^2) >= 6 restricted to periodic colorings only in the periodic sector; if chi(R^2) = 7 the conjecture is true but vacuous as a discriminator.
conjectures.novelty_claim: I believe the universally quantified statement 'every flat torus is 7-chromatic, hence all 6-colorings of the plane (if any) are aperiodic' is unstated in the literature. Nearest prior art: Townsend/Voronov (map-type colorings need 7; covers only topologically nice classes), Exoo's epsilon-unit-distance conjecture (thickened distances, no quotient structure), Polymath16 threads using tori for UPPER-bound tilings, and density bounds on tori (DeCorte-Oliveira-Vallentin school) which bound m_1, not 6-coloring feasibility per lattice. The aperiodic-monotile analogy (Smith-Myers-Kaplan-Goodman-Strauss) is rhetorical there, structural here.

conjectures.name: C2: Wrap-liberated forcing (the toroidal clamp)
conjectures.statement: There exists a lineage chi=5 UDG G (among the 12 graphs, 199-874 vertices) and a non-adjacent pair (s,t) such that the rank-1 quotient G/Zv with v = s - t (identify all vertex pairs congruent mod Zv; add every wrap edge |x - y - kv| = 1, k != 0; loop-free, i.e. no |kv| = 1) is 6-chromatic. That is: quotient identification plus the induced wrap-edge interface creates non-local forcing, which L57 proved absent under single contraction and L55 proved unreachable by single-vertex-port chains. Escalation target: a 7-chromatic toroidal UDG for some lattice L, which would prove the first 'no L-periodic 6-coloring' theorem (a new fact about the plane, since periodic colorings need not be map-type or measurable).
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Fully decidable by the experiment below: exhaustively sweep all non-adjacent pairs of S199 and L403 (and then the rest of the lineage), build each exact quotient with sympy, SAT-check 5-colorability with Cadical and re-verify any UNSAT with Glucose. All-SAT across the lineage refutes the stated (rank-1, lineage-seeded) form. Any verified UNSAT yields the first explicitly coordinatized 6-chromatic unit-distance-like quotient object.
conjectures.why_plausible: A quotient is a far larger perturbation than the L56/L57 probes: one lattice choice simultaneously contracts the whole orbit {(x, x+kv)} and installs a width >= 2 wrap interface, which is exactly the L55 route (ii) imprimitive wide-interface shape; the monoid obstruction does not apply because a lattice quotient is a global homomorphism, not a port chain. And the identification is free: no Groebner, no cocircularity miracle; the lattice IS the realization (4 continuous DOF replace the dead W3 DOF).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Unit-mod-L coincidences are codimension-1, so for generic (s,t) the quotient may contain ONLY the engineered contraction orbit and zero wrap edges; then it is a composition of contractions of FREE pairs and plausibly stays 5-chromatic for every pair, extending L57's freeness to quotient-freeness. Honest scope note: a 6-chromatic quotient by itself implies only 'no v-periodic 5-coloring' (already known); its value is mechanism proof plus a concrete imprimitive-interface specimen; new plane knowledge requires the chi >= 7 escalation.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Quotients of de Grey/Polymath chi-5 witnesses by engineered lattices as a chi-LIFTING mechanism appear nowhere I know. Polymath16 used cylinders/tori only on the upper-bound (tiling) side. Nearest repo art is Theorem R / the W3 clamp (L51-L53), of which this is the quotient-liberated shadow.

conjectures.name: C3: The defect-density dashboard (quantized failure of 6-colorings)
conjectures.statement: For an arbitrary (possibly non-measurable) 6-coloring c of R^2 let D(c) = {x : exists y, |x-y| = 1, c(y) = c(x)} and delta(c) its upper Banach density; set delta_6 = inf over all c, delta_6^per = inf over doubly periodic c. Claims: (a) delta_6 > 0 iff chi(R^2) = 7, with the quantitative form delta_6 >= c0/|V_min| where V_min is the smallest 7-chromatic UDG (sliding/averaging over isometric copies, no measurability needed); (b) delta_6^per > 0 UNCONDITIONALLY (the quantitative form of C1: periodic 6-colorings must err at a positive rate even if chi = 6); (c) for each lattice L, the LP lower bound delta_LP(L,q) (minimum fractional defect cover on the conservative cell graph of the torus at resolution q, an exact LP) converges to a positive limit, locating delta_6^per within a factor ~50 of Pritikin's upper bound of about 4e-5.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Run the defect-cover LP in cvxpy on conservative cell graphs of small tori at q = 20, 40, 80: if delta_LP(L,q) -> 0 as q grows for some L, claim (c) and the mechanism behind (b) are refuted. Conversely any explicit periodic coloring family with defect density -> 0 (e.g., a Pritikin-style construction beating every certified LP bound) refutes (b). Claim (a)'s averaging half is a short lemma the VERIFIER can formalize.
conjectures.why_plausible: Pritikin's coloring is periodic with positive defect density, and two decades of improvements (Parts-style refinements) lowered the constant but never the positivity; the dashboard explains why: positivity is forced in the periodic sector (C1) regardless of chi. The signature 'delta_6 = 0 but delta_6^per > 0' is then a checkable fingerprint of the world chi = 6 with all witnesses aperiodic, turning a metaphysical question into two density computations.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: delta_LP could genuinely decay: LP relaxations of coloring are weak (fractional defect covers can exploit non-integrality), so a positive LP limit may simply be false even if delta_6^per > 0 is true; then (c) dies while (b) survives unfalsified, and the dashboard loses its computable needle. Also the constant in (a) depends on a hypothetical V_min that may be astronomically large, making (a) quantitatively empty.
conjectures.novelty_claim: All literature I know (Pritikin 1998; Parts's Geombinatorics improvements) states UPPER bounds on the defect density of near-6-colorings. A LOWER-bound program for forced defect density, the equivalence (a), and the gap signature delta_6 = 0 < delta_6^per as a detector of chromatic aperiodicity are, to my knowledge, unstated anywhere.

conjectures.name: C4: Periodic spectral collapse (dual-lattice unblocking of the measurable thread)
conjectures.statement: For an L-periodic measurable 6-coloring, each class indicator f_i has Fourier support on the dual lattice L*, properness O(2)-averages to the exact identity sum_{xi in L*} |hat f_i(xi)|^2 J_0(2 pi |xi|) = 0, and the repo's order-2 Lasserre moment system restricted to L*-supported moments collapses from 48342 continuum variables to O(10^3) lattice variables, solvable with off-the-shelf cvxpy/SCS (no custom conic solver). Conjecture: for every lattice of covolume in [1,20] on a quadratic-field grid, the order-2 lattice-restricted SDP with the X_23 local constraints is INFEASIBLE at 6 classes, certifying that no L-periodic measurable 6-coloring exists (the measurable half of C1).
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Build the L*-restricted order-2 SDP in cvxpy for three test lattices (square, hex, one generic quadratic-irrational lattice), reusing the repo's S_k symmetry reduction and J_0 Bochner kernel. Feasibility at order 2 for any test lattice refutes the conjecture as stated (rounding the moment matrix distinguishes 'relaxation too weak' from 'a genuine periodic coloring candidate'). A by-hand sanity computation already shows the order-1 lattice LP is FEASIBLE for generic L (it needs only one dual vector with J_0(2pi|xi|) <= -alpha/(1-alpha), alpha >= 1/6), consistent with L43's order-1 weakness; so the test has real teeth exactly at order 2.
conjectures.why_plausible: Pure point spectrum historically makes Delsarte-type bounds tighter and finite (lattice packing bounds versus general packings); the periodic sector is where the blocked continuum SDP becomes an ordinary finite problem. L43 calibrates the hierarchy: order 1 provably too weak, order 2 with size-4 interaction constraints is the live frontier; restricting to L* preserves exactly those constraints while killing the variable blowup that blocks the general chi_m >= 6 run.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Order 2 may remain feasible at 6 classes for every lattice: the truth could need order 3, or the measurable periodic chromatic number could genuinely be 6 (refuting C1 in the measurable sector). SCS on marginally infeasible SDPs can also return numerically ambiguous verdicts requiring exact rational certificates the repo would still have to engineer.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Restricting the Hadwiger-Nelson moment/Lasserre hierarchy to dual-lattice spectra to DECIDE periodic measurable colorability per lattice is, to my knowledge, unstated. Nearest art: Bachoc/DeCorte/Oliveira/Vallentin torus LP-SDP bounds, which bound distance-avoiding DENSITY (m_1) on tori, never per-lattice 6-coloring feasibility, and never at order 2 with X_23-type clique constraints.

experiment.description: Toroidal wrap sweep (decides C2's stated form). New script experiments/combinatorial/toroidal_wrap_sweep.py: (1) load S199 and L403 with exact sympy coordinates via the same loaders shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py uses (UnitDistanceGraph in experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py). (2) For each non-adjacent pair (s,t): set v = coord(s) - coord(t); compute K = ceil(diam(G)/|v|) + 1; reject the pair if any k <= K has k^2*|v|^2 = 1 exactly (sympy radsimp check; loop-free condition). (3) Quotient: union-find identifying every vertex pair with x - y = kv exactly. (4) Wrap edges: for all vertex pairs and 0 < |k| <= K, numpy float prefilter on ||x - y - kv| - 1| < 1e-7, then sympy exact confirmation of |x - y - kv|^2 = 1. (5) SAT-check 5-colorability of the quotient with python-sat Cadical153; any UNSAT re-verified with Glucose4 plus a loop-free audit; cache one JSON per graph under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/toroidal/ (pair, identifications, wrap-edge count, verdict, witness coloring or core hash). Decision rule: any double-verified UNSAT = first 6-chromatic toroidal UDG, C2 confirmed, escalation to chi >= 7 quotients begins; all-SAT = C2's lineage-seeded rank-1 form refuted and L57 freeness extends to quotient-freeness (log either way as the next learning).
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything exists: exact lineage coordinates are already consumed by shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py; python-sat with Cadical and Glucose is installed and routine on 500-2000 vertex instances; sympy exact arithmetic is the house standard; numpy for the prefilter; no compiler, no new dependencies. The per-pair SAT instances are at most 199/403 vertices after quotienting, milliseconds each.
experiment.est_cost: S199: ~19.7k non-adjacent pairs; dominant cost is the vectorized O(n^2 K) wrap-edge prefilter, roughly 0.1-0.3 s per pair, so 1-2 hours; L403 comparable with sampling or overnight if exhaustive. One afternoon of coding plus one overnight run; checkpointed and resumable in the shotD style.

detector_self_check.Q2: Two independent firewalls. (i) Combinatorial side: the toroidal lower-bound objects are SEEDED by lineage graphs whose chi = 5 rests on spindle rigidity in Q(sqrt3, sqrt11); restricted to Q^2 the seeds do not exist, and quotients of the rational UDG by rational lattices remain 2-colorable, about which the mechanism claims nothing (a quotient cannot create odd structure from a bipartite-like rational graph without exact irrational wrap distances, which is precisely what the sympy gate enforces). (ii) Spectral side: the J_0 identity comes from averaging properness over the WHOLE unit circle; in Q^2 only rational unit directions carry constraints, a null subset of the circle, so the Bochner step is simply not implied. The argument visibly consumes the density of unit directions in S^1, exactly what Q^2 lacks.
detector_self_check.Linf: In L^infty the analog of C1 is FALSE (chi = 4 is attained by a periodic map coloring of half-open squares), and the proposal PREDICTS that: the entire mechanism is built on strict convexity and curvature of the Euclidean circle. The L^infty sphere has flat facets that can be aligned with tile boundaries and with a sublattice of translations, so the transversality engine behind Voronov/Exoo vanishes, and in the spectral collapse the analog of J_0 (character average over the square sphere) degenerates so that the lattice-aligned square coloring satisfies every autocorrelation constraint (verified in the 1D analog: gamma(1) = 0 for the interval 2-coloring). A mechanism that fires only under nonvanishing curvature cannot over-prove on a polyhedral norm.
detector_self_check.R1: Not rotation-blind anywhere. In R^1 the unit sphere is two points, the rotation group is finite, periodic interval colorings satisfy all constraints, and quotients R/cZ produce at worst odd-cycle effects (chi 3 for odd circumference) with no claim about chi(R) = 2. Every lower-bound mechanism here consumes either the full circle of unit directions (C1/C3/C4: equidistribution of the constraint circle against any single lattice; J_0 as a genuine O(2) average) or spindle subgraphs (C2), none of which exist on the line. A 1D port of the argument outputs nothing, as it must.
